one_line_ask: Take my approved raster abstract logo concept and produce the full hand-off package — cleaned and brightened full-colour vector master (AI/SVG), a single-colour version, a single-colour print/screen variant, a wide social-header lockup, high-res PNG/JPEG exports, and a style note with hex/RGB values and recommended typography.
brief:
I'm refreshing my brand identity and we've already landed on the strongest concept direction together: a bright, energetic, clean abstract mark — modern, no literal illustrations, no vintage flourishes. I'm handing you the approved raster rendering of that mark (PNG with some edge fringing and a slightly off-square crop from the concept render) plus a small swatch/reference image of the exact brand colours I want to lock to. Please turn this into the final production package.

Concretely: (1) straighten and tightly crop the mark so it sits square and centered; (2) normalize the tone, then push the colours so they read as bright and energetic — lift vibrance/saturation and tune the individual hues until they hit the brand harmony, not muddy; (3) knock out the background to clean transparency so the mark drops onto any header, packaging, or profile photo; (4) from that clean mark, produce the FULL-COLOUR VECTOR MASTER (AI/SVG source) so it stays razor-sharp at any size; (5) produce the SINGLE-COLOUR version (one flat brand colour on transparent) for stamps, embossing and one-ink contexts; (6) produce a single-colour PRINT/SCREEN variant (halftone/duotone) for low-ink packaging and me
